verdict_note: K491 Eblet substrate: 57.3% keystone-anchor rate at N=150 Eblets. K485 bishop-corpus baseline was 43% (N=69). K490 real-corpus baseline was 62.7% (N=870,086 tablets). The rate is within 15pp of K490 baseline (comparable).
note_corpus_difference: K491 measures keystone-anchor rate on SESSION-REASONING Eblets (abstractions of architectural decisions). K490 measured on RAW BEDROCK TABLETS (document fragments). The comparison is valid but the populations are different: session-reasoning Eblets are expected to have HIGHER keystone rates (they abstract the most notable insights).
